steps: Start with $P = N(34 - C)$. Divide both sides by N: $\frac{P}{N} = 34 - C$.
Isolate C by subtracting 34 from both sides and multiplying by −1: $C = 34 - \frac{P}{N}$.
eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — '$C + \frac{P}{N}$': This expression still contains C, so it is not a solved expression for C. ✗
  • Choice B — '$34 + \frac{P}{N}$': Wrong sign; dividing P/N = 34 − C gives C = 34 − P/N, not 34 + P/N. ✗
  • Choice C — '$34 - \frac{P}{C}$': C appears in the denominator, so C is not isolated. ✗
  • Choice D — '$34 - \frac{P}{N}$': Matches the algebra exactly: divide both sides by N, then move C to get C = 34 − P/N. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
